title: Apartments Near Case Western Reserve University
metaTitle: Apartments Near Case Western Reserve University
metaDescription: Searching for apartments near Case Western Reserve University? See University Circle transit options, nearby Cleveland rents, and voucher-friendly homes close by.
secondaryKeywords: cleveland apartments near case western reserve
answerBox: Case Western Reserve University sits in Cleveland's University Circle district on the east side, reachable by the RTA Red Line and the HealthLine bus rapid transit route on Euclid Avenue. Renters looking for apartments near CWRU typically search University Circle itself or adjacent neighborhoods like Hough, which sits just west of University Circle and is served by the same transit corridor.

sections.h2: Where is Case Western Reserve University, exactly?
body: Case Western Reserve University is located in **University Circle**, one of Cleveland's 34 officially designated Statistical Planning Areas on the city's east side. University Circle is also home to the Cleveland Museum of Art, Severance Music Center, and sits near the Cleveland Clinic's main campus — making it one of the city's denser institutional and residential districts. The area is directly served by the **RTA Red Line** (Cleveland's heavy-rail line) and the **HealthLine**, a bus-rapid-transit route running along Euclid Avenue between Public Square downtown and University Circle/East Cleveland.

Apartment inventory directly inside University Circle tends to be dominated by large purpose-built student and institutional housing. Renters who want more housing options, including standard rental homes, often look just outside the core campus footprint in adjacent east-side neighborhoods along the same transit corridor.

sections.h2: What does an apartment near University Circle cost?
body: Rents near a major institutional district can run above the citywide average because of demand from students, hospital staff, and university employees. For general Cleveland-wide context, here is what the broader market looked like as of two widely used rent-tracking sources: Zumper's median-asking-rent report (dated July 4, 2026) and RentCafe's average-rent report for professionally managed apartments (dated July 2, 2026).

sections.h2: Getting to and from campus without a car
body: The **HealthLine** runs in a dedicated center-median busway along Euclid Avenue, connecting Public Square, the Midtown corridor, University Circle, and East Cleveland — making it one of the more direct ways to reach campus from east-side neighborhoods without driving. The **RTA Red Line**, Cleveland's only heavy-rail line, also runs through the east side toward Windermere in East Cleveland, with all rail lines converging at **Tower City** downtown, where riders can transfer to the Blue and Green light-rail lines toward Shaker Heights. Renters weighing a neighborhood near campus should check its specific bus routes and walking distance to the nearest HealthLine or Red Line stop, since transit access can vary block to block even within the same general area.

Beyond rail and the HealthLine, GCRTA also runs a broader network of local bus routes and paratransit service across Cuyahoga County, so a neighborhood a few miles from campus can still be a reasonable commute if it sits along one of these lines. Anyone relying on transit rather than a car should confirm current routes and schedules directly with GCRTA before signing a lease, since service can change.

sections.h2: Voucher-friendly rental homes near University Circle
body: Rent Finder Cleveland manages rental homes in **Hough (ZIP 44103)**, an east-side neighborhood that sits between Downtown and University Circle along the same Euclid Avenue transit corridor CWRU students and staff already use. Every home we manage — including those in Hough — accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready. We don't manage traditional dormitory-style student housing, but our 2- and 3-bedroom rental homes are an option for graduate students, staff, or anyone who needs more space than a typical studio near campus.

See current listings near this corridor on our [Hough neighborhood page](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-hough-cleveland/), or [book a showing](/p/book-showing) to ask what's available close to University Circle. For medical-campus-adjacent options, our [apartments near Cleveland Clinic guide](/cleveland-rentals/apartments/apartments-near-cleveland-clinic/) covers a neighboring area.

faq.q: What neighborhood is Case Western Reserve University in?
faq.a: CWRU is in University Circle, one of Cleveland's official Statistical Planning Areas on the east side. The district also contains the Cleveland Museum of Art, Severance Music Center, and sits near the Cleveland Clinic's main campus, with direct access via the RTA Red Line and the HealthLine bus rapid transit route.

faq.q: How do I get to Case Western without a car?
faq.a: The HealthLine bus rapid transit route runs along Euclid Avenue between Public Square downtown and University Circle/East Cleveland, and the RTA Red Line heavy-rail line also serves the east side, with all rail lines meeting at Tower City downtown. Check specific stop locations against any address you're considering.

faq.q: Are there voucher-friendly apartments near Case Western?
faq.a: Rent Finder Cleveland manages Section 8-accepting rental homes in Hough (44103), an east-side neighborhood adjacent to University Circle along the same transit corridor. Availability changes, so book a showing to ask what's currently open.

faq.q: Is it cheaper to live near University Circle or elsewhere in Cleveland?
faq.a: Rents vary by building and source. Per Zumper's July 2026 report, citywide median rent for a 1-bedroom is about $1,195 and a 2-bedroom about $1,100; per RentCafe's July 2026 report, professionally managed apartment averages run higher. Areas near major institutions can price above the citywide median.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland manage student housing at CWRU?
faq.a: No. We manage standard rental homes — mostly 2- and 3-bedroom houses and duplexes — in nearby neighborhoods like Hough, not dormitory-style student housing on campus. Our homes may still suit graduate students, staff, or others who prefer more space than a typical studio.
